title: Performance, Capacity, Efficiency, and Cost Tradeoffs · Product Reasoning Brief: Unit Economics
summary: The team optimizes synthetic speed while real users face slow devices, large payloads, queue growth, resource contention, and unpredictable bills. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp2 unit economics increment in capacity and cost evidence model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Attribute fixed and variable build, run, storage, transfer, support, accessibility, and failure costs to useful product outcomes.
Bound queues and concurrency and define admission, degradation, prioritization, backpressure, shedding, retry, and recovery behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Predict Unit Economics · Theory Unit Economics
steps.instruction:
theory case — A fast average hides slow devices, large payloads, p99 queue growth, inaccessible loading states, dependency quotas, and an unbounded bill. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Attribute fixed and variable build, run, storage, transfer, support, accessibility, and failure costs to useful product outcomes.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Unit Economics contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Unit Economics · Theory Unit Economics.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for attribute fixed and variable build, run, storage, transfer, support, accessibility, and failure costs to useful product outcomes. For Predict Unit Economics · Theory Unit Economics, keep Unit Economics connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A fast average hides slow devices, large payloads, p99 queue growth, inaccessible loading states, dependency quotas, and an unbounded bill. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Attribute fixed and variable build, run, storage, transfer, support, accessibility, and failure costs to useful product outcomes.

Cheaper infrastructure automatically creates a more efficient product. That claim does not survive the predict unit economics evidence boundary.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A fast average hides slow devices, large payloads, p99 queue growth, inaccessible loading states, dependency quotas, and an unbounded bill. The worked example variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Attribute fixed and variable build, run, storage, transfer, support, accessibility, and failure costs to useful product outcomes. A defensible solution applies Unit Economics, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to capacity and cost evidence model.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-4
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
